name: Peach Butter
recipeIngredient: 2 Quarts peeled, pitted and chopped very ripe peaches
1 cup (simmered down from 2 cups) concentrated white grape juice
1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon grated lemon rind (or to taste)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place all ingredients (except almond extract) in a large, nonaluminum kettle.
2. Cook over low heat until thick, stirring frequently and skimming if necessary.
3. When suitably thick, taste the mixture. Add the almond extract if desired.
4. Pour into hot, clean jars and cap with hot lids firmly screwed down.
5. Process in boiling water bath for 10 minutes after water has returned to boiling, or in pressure cooker for 10 minutes at 5 pounds pressure.
6. To freeze, cool to room temperature, then place in the sharp freeze section of the freezer until hard.
7. Remove to the main freezer for storage, or keep refrigerated and it will be good for 10 days or more.
YIELD: Makes 6 to 7 cups
Nutritional value: 2 tablespoons contain 20 calories. 4 tablespoons equal 1 diabetic fruit exchange. If no salt is added, 4 tablespoons contain less than 1 milligram of sodium.
